source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Zig/Rust)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Syndica supplies the most critical applications in Web3 with enterprise-grade RPC infrastructure and developer tools tailored for the Solana ecosystem. Joining our team means you'll be held to a high standard, technically challenged, and grow close to a group of individuals passionate about building new infrastructure technologies.



We are backed by strategic partners, investors, and advisors who are all-in on our mission: Chamath Palihapitiya of Social Capital, Steve Jang of Kindred Ventures, Joe McCann of Asymmetric, Jump Crypto, Coinbase Ventures, Solana Ventures, Circle Ventures, and many more.




ABOUT YOU:

 - You are a talented engineer who thrives in a collaborative and fast-paced environment.  

 - You're excited about contributing to the decentralization of blockchain ecosystems.

 - You have 3+ years of related professional experience using Rust (experience in memory-managed languages (Zig/C/C++) is nice to have but not required).

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or relevant experience.





ABOUT THE ROLE:

As a Senior Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in designing and implementing a high-performance Solana validator client from scratch. 



The team you’d be joining is building a new open-source Solana validator client, Sig, using the Zig programming language. You can find more technical details about the project in the original announcement here https://blog.syndica.io/introducing-sig-by-syndica-an-rps-focused-solana-validator-client-written-in-zig/.



The role’s primary responsibility will involve understanding the Agave validator implementation codebase, written in Rust, and translating, optimizing, and improving the implementation in Zig (take a peek at the current work here https://github.com/syndica/sig/!).



Key responsibilities: 

 - Understanding and Communicating Rust Code: mapping and accurately communicating how the existing Rust client works.

 - Writing high-quality code in Zig: translating the Rust code into Zig while maintaining high-quality code, including performance and readability.

 - Performance Research: iteratively analyzing and optimizing software bottlenecks in Zig while leveraging high-performance tools such as SIMD operations to achieve performance requirements.

 - Code Ownership: lead the implementation, benchmarking, and testing of your validator components from start to finish.

 - Collaboration: leveraging strong communication skills to break down complex ideas into simple terms and demonstrating a knack for collaboration across a large group of engineers.




WHAT DOES SUCCESS IN THIS ROLE LOOK LIKE?

 - In three months, you have become one of our core Zig developers. You’ll have taken on independent code review responsibilities and are collaborating on the design of new features.

 - In six months, you have earned the trust of the team and are delivering tasks through the entire SDLC, from design through development with minimal guidance, and are helping to effectively mentor new engineers joining the team.

 - In twelve months, you have established a cadence of predictable, on-time delivery without cutting corners.
   


WANT TO STAND OUT? 

Contribute to the open-source Sig codebase here https://github.com/syndica/sig/!

source_jobs.title: Research Manager
source_jobs.description:
About us:

Syndica supplies the most critical applications in Web3 with enterprise-grade RPC infrastructure and developer tools tailored for the Solana ecosystem. Joining our team means you'll be held to a high standard, technically challenged, and grow close to a group of individuals passionate about building new infrastructure technologies.



We are backed by strategic partners, investors, and advisors who are all-in on our mission: Chamath Palihapitiya of Social Capital, Steve Jang of Kindred Ventures, Joe McCann of Asymmetric, Jump Crypto, Coinbase Ventures, Solana Ventures, Circle Ventures, and many more.



About you:

 - 4+ years of professional experience in an academic-grade crypto research publishing house, conducting VC/IB/PE-style emerging technologies market analysis at an investment firm focused on crypto, or corresponding analytical domains.

 - An ability to think critically about blockchain-based businesses and their positioning in the broader context of both onchain and real-world markets.

 - Strong organizational skills, including the ability to manage multiple moving parts, set deadlines, coordinate feedback loops, and drive written deliverables to completion with a strong sense of ownership.

 - A collaborative ethos and a willingness to work closely with technical Research Engineers to draw out insights from onchain data. An understanding of data science concepts and an intermediary grasp of statistical analysis is required.

 - A willingness to roll up your sleeves and get things done, rolling with the punches as needed. Experience working in a remote or highly distributed team environment is a plus.



About the role:

Syndica’s Research function launched in 2023 with the aim of delivering crisp, insightful crypto analysis to the public. Since then we have established a reputation for detailed reports on a range of sectors within crypto, and our reports have quickly become a standard-bearer for knowledge creation in the industry. If you have not already, visit blog.syndica.io/tag/research/ http://blog.syndica.io/tag/research/ to make yourself familiar with Syndica’s research reports.



The Research Manager role is best understood as a ‘Product Manager’ for the Research function - the role encompasses both day-to-day execution and medium-term strategic thinking.



As a Research Manager you have a broad mandate to ensure the research function is achieving its objectives. On a day-to-day basis this means that you will work hand-in-hand with our Research Engineers to develop their sector-specific reports. You will think big picture about each crypto sector we write on, help drive analysis and draw out interesting insights, suggest areas of further inquiry for our Research Engineers, and coordinate feedback from relevant Syndica team members, including our Head of Research. You will have the opportunity to own special research projects of your own, with other members of the research team available to assist as appropriate.



You’ll also be responsible for keeping research workflows organized and on-track—leading daily huddles, maintaining content trackers, setting timelines, and monitoring ecosystem channels (Telegram, Twitter, Email) to surface relevant insights. You will manage the editorial process from first draft to final publication—reviewing and refining copy, incorporating feedback from research leads, and publishing finalized reports.



In the medium term, you will bring a fresh perspective to our publishing processes, ideate new initiatives to maximize reach and distribution, and shape innovative research projects. You will build your own point-of-view about crypto to help guide the research team’s direction as it grows. You will offer strategic thinking about each of our individual reports and help drive their evolution over time. You will collaborate with Syndica’s Infrastructure and Open Source teams on Research as-needed, gathering feedback from domain experts, leveraging proprietary data

source_jobs.title: Revenue Growth Manager (B2B Sales)
source_jobs.description:
About us:

Syndica supplies the most critical applications in Web3 with enterprise-grade RPC infrastructure and developer tools tailored for the Solana ecosystem. Joining our team means you'll be held to a high standard, technically challenged, and grow close to a group of individuals passionate about building new infrastructure technologies.



We are backed by strategic partners, investors, and advisors who are all-in on our mission: Chamath Palihapitiya of Social Capital, Steve Jang of Kindred Ventures, Joe McCann of Asymmetric, Jump Crypto, Coinbase Ventures, Solana Ventures, Circle Ventures, and many more.



About you:

 - 5+ years of relevant professional experience in strategic revenue growth or B2B sales within a SaaS company.

 - Strong understanding of developer infrastructure, particularly within the Web3 and crypto ecosystem.

 - Familiarity with the needs, workflows, and pain points of a developer-focused audience.

 - Proven ability to write, test, and iterate on copy, creative assets, and distribution strategies.

 - Working knowledge of data analytics tools to track, evaluate, and optimize campaign performance.

 - Demonstrated experience scaling growth channels and driving revenue in a SaaS B2B environment.

 - Comfortable engaging directly with decision makers at B2B organizations, with a track record of successfully educating and onboarding them onto SaaS platforms.

 - A systematic problem-solving mindset with a strong sense of ownership and execution.

 - Experience working in a remote or highly distributed team environment is a plus.



About the role:

The Growth Manager will be responsible for driving user engagement and revenue growth across the platform, with a particular focus on creatively unlocking new opportunities within a highly targeted and limited market. This role owns the development and execution of a go-to-market strategy aimed at attracting, converting, and retaining developers and strategic partners. Success requires a blend of analytical rigor and unconventional thinking, someone who can test and iterate rapidly across paid channels, optimize conversion at every stage of the funnel, and craft inventive, full-funnel experiences that deliver measurable business impact.



What does success in this role look like?

 - In three months, you have become our go-to teammate on all things Growth, with a holistic understanding of our value proposition. You’ve implemented a number of growth experiments and have narrowed in on promising channels.

 - In six months, you have earned the trust of the team to drive outcomes, from ideation through implementation with minimal guidance, and are delivering industry-best MoM growth and positive ROAS.

 - In twelve months, you have attained a mastery of growth initiatives, and have created a system to continuously ideate, experiment, and deliver results.

source_jobs.title: Don't See A Perfect Fit?
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Syndica supplies the most critical applications in Web3 with enterprise-grade RPC infrastructure and developer tools tailored for the Solana ecosystem. Joining our team means you'll be held to a high standard, technically challenged, and grow close to a group of individuals passionate about building new infrastructure technologies.



We are backed by strategic partners, investors, and advisors who are all-in on our mission: Chamath Palihapitiya of Social Capital, Steve Jang of Kindred Ventures, Joe McCann of Asymmetric, Jump Crypto, Coinbase Ventures, Solana Ventures, Circle Ventures, and many more.



Responsibilities

 - Help us build the next generation of Web 3.0 developer infrastructure!



Qualifications

 - Entrepreneurial spirit / ability to roll up your sleeves and try different things

 - Ability to collaborate across multiple functions driving toward a common goal

 - Loves and understands technology. Stays up-to-date on competition and disruptors in the market

 - Align a portion of your day with the business hours of Central Time Zone - UTC -6

 - Systematic problem-solving approach, combined with a strong sense of ownership and drive

notes: Syndica is a Web3 infrastructure company with a strong engineering focus.
